summary: Iteration 33: Deep investigation of asset delete bug. Backend API works correctly (POST /api/proxy/engin/delete returns success). Frontend issue: setAllData state updater is called and executes correctly (verified via console.log showing 500→499), but React does NOT re-render the component with the new state. Toast shows success message, but asset remains in list and count stays at 500.

critical.issue: Asset delete: React state update (setAllData) executes but component does not re-render

passed_tests: Backend: POST /api/proxy/engin/delete with numeric ID returns {result:[{typeMsg:'success'}]}
Frontend: Click trash icon shows confirmation modal (data-testid='single-delete-modal')
Frontend: Click 'Supprimer' button triggers API call
Frontend: Toast appears with success message (data-testid='delete-toast')
failed_tests: Frontend: Asset count does NOT decrease from 500 to 499 after delete
Frontend: Deleted asset is NOT removed from the list
Frontend: setAllData state update does NOT trigger re-render
action_items: CRITICAL: Investigate why React setAllData does not trigger re-render in PremiumAssets.jsx
Check if there's a parent component or Redux store that's overwriting the local allData state
Consider using useReducer instead of useState for complex state updates
Check React version for known bugs with useState in async functions
Verify that the component is not being unmounted/remounted during the delete operation
critical_code_review_comments: handleSingleDelete at line ~192: State updater setAllData(currentData => currentData.filter(...)) executes correctly but React does not re-render
The component uses both local state (allData) and Redux state (engines from getEngines selector) - potential conflict
loadData function uses dispatch(fetchEngines) which updates Redux, then sets local allData - this dual-state pattern may cause issues
Consider consolidating to use only Redux state OR only local state, not both
context_for_next_testing_agent: Asset delete bug is NOT fixed. The API call succeeds, the state updater function executes correctly (verified via console.log showing array length going from 500 to 499), but React does NOT re-render the component. The toast shows success but the asset remains in the list. This is a React state management issue, not an API issue. The component uses a dual-state pattern (local useState + Redux) which may be causing conflicts.

rca_of_the_issue.root_cause: React useState setAllData updater function executes but does not trigger component re-render
reproduction_steps: 4. Click trash icon on any asset card
6. Observe: Toast shows success, but asset remains in list
verified_working: State updater returns filtered array (499 items)
not_working: Component does not re-render after setAllData
Deleted asset still visible in first position
potential_causes: Dual-state pattern (local useState + Redux getEngines) causing conflicts
Parent component re-render resetting local state
React batching issue with multiple setState calls
Stale closure capturing old state reference
rca_of_the_issue.suggested_fix: Consolidate state management - either use only Redux OR only local state, not both. If using local state, ensure no parent component or Redux subscription is overwriting it.
